title: Billing
lede: iMash runs on a prepaid balance — top it up once and every call, message, and AI action draws from it automatically. This guide walks you through adding a payment method, funding your balance, and tracing every cent in the Ledger.

steps.title: Open the Billing page
steps.content:
Everything money-related lives on one page: your balance, payment methods, top-ups, the full transaction ledger, and your phone-number subscriptions.

  1. In the top navigation bar, click Billing (or go directly to /billing).
  2. The page title reads Billing & Usage with a billing-mode selector next to it. Today everything runs on Prepaid Balance; Pay as you go is shown as Coming Soon and can't be selected yet.
  3. The layout has three areas: the payment methods card on the left, the Prepaid Balance / Add Funds card on the right, and the Ledger / Phone Number Order tabs below.
💡 Your prepaid balance is what keeps agents answering and campaigns dialing — if it hits $0, usage-based services pause. Check it here whenever in doubt.

images.alt: Billing & Usage page showing payment method form, prepaid balance and ledger
images.caption: The Billing page: payment methods on the left, balance and Add Funds on the right, Ledger below

images.alt: Billing & Usage page showing payment method form, prepaid balance and ledger
images.caption: The Billing page: payment methods on the left, balance and Add Funds on the right, Ledger below

steps.title: Add a payment method
steps.content:
Before you can add funds you need at least one saved payment method. If you have none yet, the form opens automatically.

  1. In the left card, choose Card or US bank account.
  2. For a card, fill in Card number, Expiration date, Security code, Country, and ZIP code. For a bank account, follow the on-screen bank-connection flow instead.
  3. Click Add Payment Method. The saved method appears in the Existing Payment Methods list.
  4. To save more methods later, click Add Another Payment Method; use Cancel to close the form without saving.
  5. In the list, tick the checkbox next to a method to select it for top-ups (it shows (Selected)), or click the trash icon to remove a method you no longer use.

Your card details are stored securely by our payment processor (Stripe) — iMash never sees your full card number.

⚠️ If you select a US bank account, a note reminds you that bank payments may take a few days to process — plan top-ups ahead so your balance never runs dry mid-campaign.

images.alt: Add Payment Method form with Card and US bank account options
images.caption: Choose Card or US bank account, fill in the details, then click Add Payment Method

images.alt: Add Payment Method form with Card and US bank account options
images.caption: Choose Card or US bank account, fill in the details, then click Add Payment Method

steps.title: Add funds to your balance
steps.content:
The right-hand card shows your live Prepaid Balance and the Add Funds controls beneath it.

  1. Make sure the payment method you want to charge is selected (checkbox in the left card).
  2. In the Add Funds field, type the dollar amount you want to add.
  3. Click Add Funds. Once the payment is confirmed, your balance updates on the spot.

Processing fees, in plain terms:

  • Credit/debit card top-ups carry a 3% processing fee — your card is charged the full amount you entered and your balance is credited 3% less. As soon as you type an amount, the page spells it out: "You'll be charged $X and $Y will be credited to your balance."
  • Bank account (ACH) top-ups have no fee — the full amount is credited.
💡 Topping up larger amounts less often means fewer card fees — or use a bank account and skip the fee entirely.

images.alt: Prepaid Balance card with Add Funds amount field and buttons
images.caption: Type an amount and click Add Funds — the fee note above explains exactly what will be credited

images.alt: Prepaid Balance card with Add Funds amount field and buttons
images.caption: Type an amount and click Add Funds — the fee note above explains exactly what will be credited

steps.title: Review your card and bank payments
steps.content:
Every top-up you've ever made is one click away.

  1. Next to the Add Funds button, click View Transactions.
  2. The Transaction History window lists each payment with its Date, Amount, Status (e.g. SUCCEEDED), and Description.
  3. Close the window when you're done — nothing here is editable, it's your payment receipt trail.

This list shows the money you paid in through Stripe. For how the balance was spent, use the Ledger tab (next step).

images.alt: Transaction History modal with Date, Amount, Status and Description columns
images.caption: Transaction History — every card and bank payment with its status

images.alt: Transaction History modal with Date, Amount, Status and Description columns
images.caption: Transaction History — every card and bank payment with its status

steps.title: Open the Ledger and set your filters
steps.content:
The Ledger tab is your money microscope — every credit and debit on the account, filterable from every angle. Scroll down and make sure the Ledger tab is active (it is by default).

The filter card at the top controls everything below it:

  • View — Single user shows one account's ledger; All users combined merges every account you can see (you, your team's sub-accounts, and referred accounts if you're an affiliate).
  • Combined view — with "All users combined" selected, switch between Summary & per-user (totals and a per-user table) and Activity feed (a live stream of every entry).
  • Date range — limit everything to a start/end date.
  • Show — filter by entry type: All, Deposits, Transfers, Usage, or Commission.
  • Service type — narrow usage rows to specific services (Inbound Calls, Outbound Calls, Web Calls, SMS Inbound/Outbound, AI Email Writer, and more). You can pick several at once.

images.alt: Ledger filter card with View, Combined view, Date range, Show and Service type filters
images.caption: The Ledger filter card — view mode, date range, entry type, and service type

images.alt: Ledger filter card with View, Combined view, Date range, Show and Service type filters
images.caption: The Ledger filter card — view mode, date range, entry type, and service type

steps.title: Read the Activity feed
steps.content:
The Activity feed (the default view) is a newest-first stream of every billing event across all users in your scope. Each row shows:

  • When — date and exact time; hover for the full timestamp with timezone.
  • Type — a colored pill: Deposit (money you paid in), Admin credit (credit granted by an administrator), Transfer (balance moved between accounts), Usage (spend on calls, messages, AI), Commission (margin or affiliate earnings credited to you), and Manual Transfer (an off-ledger balance adjustment the system detected and labeled for you).
  • User — whose balance moved, with a View ledger shortcut that jumps straight to that user's single-user ledger.
  • Description — what happened: the service and call duration for usage, the counterparty for transfers, the payment reference for deposits.
  • Service — the service type pill for usage and commission rows.
  • Amount — green with an up arrow for money in, red with a down arrow for money out.

Use the Prev / Next buttons under the table to walk further back in time, and Refresh to pull the latest entries.

💡 When a call generates both a usage debit and a commission credit, a small blue timeline rail on the left edge visually connects the related rows — so you can see exactly which call fed which commission.

images.alt: Activity feed table with Deposit, Admin credit, Commission and Usage rows
images.caption: The Activity feed — deposits, credits, commissions and usage in one stream

images.alt: Activity feed table with Deposit, Admin credit, Commission and Usage rows
images.caption: The Activity feed — deposits, credits, commissions and usage in one stream

steps.title: See totals with Summary & per-user
steps.content:
Want the big picture instead of a stream? Switch Combined view to Summary & per-user.

  1. The summary card draws proportional bars for Usage by service, Earnings by service, Earnings by role, and Paid Deposits — each with its total for the selected date range.
  2. Below it, the Per-user breakdown table lists every account you can see with its Balance, Paid Deposits (real money in via Stripe), Commission (what that account earned you), Usage (what it spent), and Last activity.
  3. Sub-accounts are nested under their main account — click the + on a main's row to expand its subs. A collapsed main also rolls its subs' commission up into a "Subs" line.
  4. Click View ledger on any row to jump into that user's full single-user ledger.

images.alt: Summary breakdown bars and per-user breakdown table
images.caption: Summary & per-user — service breakdowns on top, one row per account below

images.alt: Summary breakdown bars and per-user breakdown table
images.caption: Summary & per-user — service breakdowns on top, one row per account below

steps.title: Drill into one account with Single user view
steps.content:
The Single user view is where you audit one account cent by cent.

  1. Set View to Single user.
  2. Pick the account in the User dropdown — it's searchable and grouped by scope: You, your Tenant (mains and subs listed separately), and Referred accounts if you're an affiliate. Each option shows the account's current balance.
  3. The summary card shows that user's own Usage by service, Earnings by service, Earnings by role, and Paid Deposits bars.
  4. The Ledger table below lists every entry for that account. The Show and Service type filters apply here too.
💡 In single-user view the ledger validates the balance chain: if the balance ever moved outside the normal entries, a gold Manual Transfer row is inserted to explain the gap — so the numbers always add up in front of you.

images.alt: Single user ledger with user picker, summary bars and full entry list
images.caption: Single user view — pick an account, see its breakdowns and its complete ledger

images.alt: Single user ledger with user picker, summary bars and full entry list
images.caption: Single user view — pick an account, see its breakdowns and its complete ledger

steps.title: Manage phone-number subscriptions
steps.content:
The Phone Number Order tab lists every phone number you rent through iMash, each with its own monthly subscription.

  • Columns: Phone Number (with country tag), Owner (which main or sub bought it), Status (Active, Trial, Payment Due, Past Due, Cancelling with days left, or Released), Next Billing date, Price per month, Purchased date, Last Used (when a call last used the number — "Never" flags idle numbers worth releasing), and Action.
  • The Active / All toggle above the table hides or shows released (historical) numbers.

To stop paying for a number:

  1. Click Cancel on its row and confirm with Cancel Subscription. The number stays active until the end of the paid billing period — the Status column counts down the days.
  2. Changed your mind? Click Undo before the period ends and the subscription keeps renewing.
  3. Need it gone now? On a cancelling row, click Delete — the number is released immediately, with no refund for the remaining period.
⚠️ Releasing a number is permanent — once released it goes back to the carrier pool and may not be available to re-buy. You can also tick several rows and use the bulk Cancel / Undo / Delete buttons that appear above the table.

images.alt: Phone Number Order tab with Active/All toggle and subscription columns
images.caption: Phone Number Order — every rented number with its status, price, and next billing date

images.alt: Phone Number Order tab with Active/All toggle and subscription columns
images.caption: Phone Number Order — every rented number with its status, price, and next billing date

steps.title: Billing for team members and affiliates
steps.content:
A few billing abilities live outside this page but connect straight to it:

  • Team members you invite don't get billing access by default. From User Management you can grant a member billing access, set a per-member per-minute price, or transfer balance from your account to theirs — every transfer shows up in the Ledger as a Transfer row on both sides.
  • Affiliates get an extra Withdrawals tab on this page (between Ledger and Phone Number Order) where you can request a cash-out of your accumulated commission balance and track each request's status.
  • The Commission entries in your Ledger show exactly where affiliate and sub-account earnings came from — down to the specific call.

That's the whole money loop: fund it once, and iMash accounts for every cent in the open.

nextSteps.description: Your balance is loaded — now put it to work and keep an eye on where it goes.

support.content:

  • 📧 Email us at support@imash.io
